title: 3 Consumer Defensive Stocks for the Value Investor
text: If you want to make your portfolio more resilient to recessions, the following defensive stocks may be suitable options. These businesses have historically continued to generate earnings and dividends during hard economic times as they offer goods and services on which people do not typically cut their spending even in periods of financial distress.

title: Is Trending Stock Tyson Foods, Inc. (TSN) a Buy Now?
text: Zacks.com users have recently been watching Tyson (TSN) quite a bit. Thus, it is worth knowing the facts that could determine the stock's prospects.

title: Why Tyson (TSN) Could Beat Earnings Estimates Again
text: Tyson (TSN) has an impressive earnings surprise history and currently possesses the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely beat in its next quarterly report.

title: Tyson Foods: A Great Defensive Stock
text: Dividend yield of 2.14%. In the consumer defensive sector, good for times of economic uncertainty.

title: Tyson Foods' (TSN) Latest Move to Fuel International Expansion
text: Tyson Foods (TSN) to acquire a 15% equity stake in Tanmiah subsidiary Agriculture Development Company and a 60% equity stake in Supreme Foods Processing Company.

title: Tyson Foods expands in Middle East with stakes in Saudi companies
text: Tyson Foods Inc. TSN, +0.63% announced Wednesday that it has taken a 15% equity stake in Agriculture Development Company (ADC), a subsidiary of Saudi Arabia-based Tanmiah Food Company, and a 60% equity stake in Supreme Foods Processing Company. Further financial details of the partnerships were not disclosed.

title: Tyson Foods Is Another Attractive Dividend Growth Company
text: Tyson Foods has been paying dividends without reducing them for more than 25 years. Shares of Tyson Foods are trading for less than 10 times earnings.
